flashcards.definition: H₂O consists of two hydrogen atoms bonded to one oxygen atom with a bent molecular shape; polar covalent bonds allow hydrogen bonding.
Weak interactions between the partially positive hydrogen of one water molecule and the partially negative oxygen of another; gives water unique properties.
Cohesion: water molecules sticking to each other; Adhesion: water molecules sticking to other surfaces; responsible for surface tension and capillary action.
Water has maximum density at 4°C; ice floats because it's less dense than liquid water; denser objects sink, less dense objects float.
Water has high specific heat (4.18 J/g°C), meaning it requires much energy to change temperature; moderates Earth's climate.
Evaporation: liquid water converts to vapor (absorbs heat); Condensation: water vapor converts to liquid (releases heat).
Pure water has pH 7 (neutral); pH < 7 is acidic, pH > 7 is basic; water ionizes as H₂O ⇌ H⁺ + OH⁻.
Water is the universal solvent; polar nature allows it to dissolve many ionic and polar compounds essential for life.
Movement of water across a semipermeable membrane from area of higher water concentration to lower water concentration; vital for cell function.
Process where water molecules surround and interact with ions or other molecules; essential for dissolving salts and sustaining aquatic life.
